items.headline: Trump Says USA in 'Heated' Iran Talks
items.summary: President Donald Trump said he was in 'heated negotiations' involving the war with Iran.

items.headline: Trump Says Iran Could 'Die Tonight'
items.summary: President Donald Trump threatened Iran with civilizational destruction.

items.headline: Viral research firm Citrini sent an analyst to the Strait of Hormuz. From smugglers to Iranian drones, here's what they found.
items.summary: Citrini Research's "Analyst #3" went to the Strait of Hormuz with recording equipment, "a pack of Cuban cigars, $15,000 in cash and a roll of Zyn."

items.headline: Iran Is Not Blocking the Strait of Hormuz. It's Running a Toll Booth

items.headline: Trump's shipping waiver does not boost oil flows within US; fuel exports soar
items.summary: Last month, Trump waived Jones Act limitations for 60 days starting March 17, hoping the move would help tame the ‌surge in fuel prices caused by the Iran war by increasing shipments from the US Gulf Coast to other coastal markets in the country

items.headline: What is Benjamin Netanyahu’s end game in the Iran war?
items.summary: The war in the Middle East is a huge political book for the Israeli prime minister in an election year – but it may come at a great cost.

items.headline: Sign Of The Times
items.summary: If you haven’t seen the movie, Project Hail Mary, then you should… it is excellent. It is faithful to Andy Weir’s 2021 book, hilarious and heart-warming, and with the Artemis II crew circling the moon, the film is just what we needed.

items.headline: 'Painful' interest rates are looming with the world facing a new wave of inflationary pressure, Harvard economist says
items.summary: Kenneth Rogoff said the latest oil-price spike is adding to a host of inflationary pressures facing the global economy.

items.headline: Australia Fuel Prices Ease Slightly to ~$2.40/L After Excise Cuts, But Diesel Soars Amid Iran War Fallout
items.summary: SYDNEY — Australian fuel prices have begun to moderate in early April 2026 after the federal government's temporary halving of the fuel excise and an additional cut funded by GST revenue, yet they remain significantly higher than pre-conflict levels due to on…

items.headline: Russia Expands Gasoline Export Ban, Faces Major Oil Outages Due to Drone Strikes
items.summary: The Russian government has expanded a ban on gasoline exports to include producers of the fuel, according to a report from Interfax news agency [1]. The ban, which now includes all gasoline producers, is scheduled to remain in effect until July 31. It expands…

items.headline: Here’s a List of Gulf Energy Infrastructure Damaged in Iran War
items.summary: Dozens of refineries, oil fields, gas plants, ports and other energy infrastructure have been damaged by missile and drone strikes, five weeks into the Iran war. US President Donald Trump threatened to bring “Hell” to Iran with strikes on power …

items.headline: Ukraine Steps Up Drone Attacks on Russian Oil Infrastructure
items.summary: The key Russian oil export terminal at Novorossiysk was on fire early on Monday following an overnight drone attack, as Ukraine intensifies strikes on Russia’s oil infrastructure to reduce its export capabilities amid surging oil prices. The area at the port …

items.headline: Three killed in Russian strike on Odesa as Ukraine hits Russian oil sites
items.summary: Russian drone strikes in Odesa claimed civilian lives including a toddler. Ukrainian drones retaliated targeting Russia's oil export ports.  The attacks damaged infrastructure and injured civilians. Ukraine faces ongoing challenges with air defense.  Russia c…

items.headline: Trump's 'English Obscenities' to Iran - Latest War News

items.headline: IRGC intelligence chief Majid Khademi killed in Tehran strikes
items.summary: Majid Khademi, the IRGC intelligence chief, was killed in U.S.-Israeli strikes on Tehran. He was a career security insider who rose through Iran's counterintelligence system and helped oversee repression and surveillance.

items.headline: Iran offers potential 45-day ceasefire with U.S. mediated by Pakistan
items.summary: Iran and the United States are discussing the terms of a potential 45-day ceasefire that could open the door to a permanent end to the war. Pakistan is mediating between the U.S. and Iran for high-level negotiations.

dimension_signal.rationale: Trump engages in serious ceasefire negotiations showing reduced impulsive escalation

items.headline: IMF imposes 11 new conditions on Pakistan bailout over India tensions
items.summary: The IMF imposed 11 new conditions on Pakistan to release the next tranche of its $7 billion bailout package. Geopolitical tensions with India may jeopardize Pakistan's fiscal reform targets.

items.headline: Xi Jinping invites KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wen to Beijing amid Taiwan tensions
items.summary: CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping invited KMT Chairwoman Cheng Li-wen to lead a delegation to Beijing from April 7-12. The timing coincides with frequent PLA activities near Taiwan and military budget discussions.

dimension_signal.rationale: Xi demonstrates commitment to peaceful reunification through opposition party engagement

items.headline: NABU exposes new $17.7 million grain corruption scheme in Ukraine
items.summary: Ukrainian anti-corruption agencies uncovered an alleged corruption scheme involving the embezzlement of $17.7 million worth of grain. The State Food and Grain Corporation allegedly transferred control of shipments without payment from foreign buyers.

dimension_signal.rationale: Continued NABU investigations expose ongoing corruption issues in Zelensky administration

items.headline: SIPRI reports NATO reduces reliance on U.S. arms imports amid Trump threats
items.summary: European NATO nations reduced U.S. arms imports from 64% to 58% in 2021-2025. Trump's threats against allies and policy shift toward American homeland defense raised doubts about U.S. reliability as ally.

dimension_signal.rationale: European allies reducing defense dependence on U.S. shows erosion of Trump's alliance leadership

items.headline: IAEA Director General confirms Iran nuclear facilities increasingly inaccessible
items.summary: IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi confirmed significant damage to Iran's nuclear facilities. While there has been no evidence of Iran building a nuclear bomb, its large stockpile of near-weapons grade uranium and refusal to grant full access are causes for concern.

items.headline: Modi declares India will not tolerate nuclear blackmail after Operation Sindoor
items.summary: Prime Minister Modi announced that India will not tolerate nuclear blackmail and that Operation Sindoor represents India's new policy against terrorism. He stated future operations depend on Pakistan's behavior.

dimension_signal.rationale: Modi explicitly rejects nuclear coercion showing hardened position on national security

items.headline: PLA leadership purge continues as Xi removes military officials from legislative bodies
items.summary: The PRC removed numerous current and former PLA officers from state legislative bodies leading up to annual meetings as part of ongoing purge campaign. Xi Jinping is targeting those perceived as having insufficient loyalty within the PLA.

dimension_signal.rationale: Xi continues systematic purge of military leadership to ensure loyalty and control

items.headline: Iran forms human chains to protect power plants ahead of Trump deadline
items.summary: Iranian state media showed human chains forming in front of the Kazerun combined cycle power plant ahead of President Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face destruction of power plants.

dimension_signal.rationale: Trump sets ultimatums with specific deadlines for civilian infrastructure destruction

items.headline: Ukraine corruption scandal reaches Zelensky's inner circle as chief of staff Yermak resigns
items.summary: Ukrainian Energy Minister German Galushchenko was arrested while attempting to flee to Poland, and Zelensky's influential chief of staff Andrey Yermak resigned amid the $100 million corruption investigation. The scandal highlights tensions between anti-corruption agencies and the SBU.

dimension_signal.rationale: Senior officials in Zelensky's inner circle arrested and resigned over corruption charges
